prompt: Play this short World 3 position-sensitive unopened-pot chain.

steps.prompt: Step 1: Hero is on the button and villain is in the hijack. Who will be in position after the flop?
steps.feedback_correct_v1: Correct. The button acts after the hijack postflop, so hero keeps position.
steps.feedback_incorrect_v1: Incorrect. The button acts after the hijack postflop, so hero is in position here.
steps.why_v1: Position changes how much freedom a hand gets before and after the flop.

steps.prompt: Step 2: Keep the same seats. Hero holds KJo on the button and the pot is unopened. Which simple preflop action fits better now?
steps.feedback_correct_v1: Correct. The same middling broadway hand becomes a clear open when hero gets late position and an unopened pot.
steps.feedback_incorrect_v1: Incorrect. In this late-position unopened frame, KJo is strong enough to open rather than pass.
steps.why_v1: Late position lets middling broadway hands attack an unopened pot more profitably.

steps.prompt: Step 3: Now keep the same KJo, but move hero to the hijack in an unopened pot with villain on the button behind. Which simple preflop action fits better?
steps.feedback_correct_v1: Correct. The hand did not change, but the earlier seat did. Without late-position leverage, this is a disciplined fold.
steps.feedback_incorrect_v1: Incorrect. The same hand does not keep the same value when hero loses late position in the unopened pot.
steps.recap_v1: World 3 keeps getting more practical: the same hand can open comfortably late and still fold earlier when position stops doing the heavy lifting.
steps.why_v1: Preflop hand value comes from both the cards and the seat that has to use them.
